framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver hangs
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh <<'EOF' ... EOF (with canned answers for org/sponsored_poc/web/full/typescript). Process never returns even after >2min; multiple parallel sweep agents show same hang in ps output.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh should either accept --non-interactive flags or reliably consume stdin answers and exit
framework_bugs.actual: Process spins indefinitely; stdin appears not to be drained past tool resolution / install confirmation prompts. Documented in runbook as expected fallback path.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh lacks --project, --platform, --track, --deployment, --governance, --language, --project-dir flags. Without scriptable inputs, automated UAT/CI cannot exercise the discovery flow end-to-end.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh (top of file) and runbook RUNBOOK.md Section A 'Init driver — important' note already calls this out as expected gap.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 exit=0; phase1_architecture appended to process-state.json

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is array

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed exit=0 in order

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh reports 'Phase gates consistent.' exit=0

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: Phase advance is manual via approval log + jq; framework does not auto-bump current_phase. Set current_phase=2 directly to proceed (matches framework design — gate validates consistency, advance is a governance act).

documentation_gaps.gap: check-phase-gate.sh validates consistency but never advances .current_phase. The runbook's 'phase_state_advances_to_2' assertion implies an advance command but none exists. Builder needs explicit guidance: edit phase-state.json manually after gate passes + governance approval.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh + docs/builders-guide.md (no clear 'how to advance phase' section found)

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit succeeded after first 5 steps + check-commit-message exit=0

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: After resetting build_loop to empty state, pre-commit-gate.sh returned permissionDecision=deny with 'no Build Loop active' reason for 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' (also note: required phase2_init steps to be marked complete first, or gate denies with 'phase 2 init not verified')

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote valid .claude/pending-approval.json; pre-commit-gate.sh returned permissionDecision=deny with rich pending-approval reason including question/options/recommendation

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: pending-approval.sh --resolve cleared sentinel; subsequent commit gate exit=0

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .features_completed = ['uat-feat-1'] in build-progress.json

framework_bugs.summary: test-gate.sh --record-feature: integer comparison error on null test_interval
framework_bugs.reproduction: cd <project>; bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/test-gate.sh --record-feature 'uat-feat-1' (with no test_interval set in project config)
framework_bugs.expected: Clean output reporting the recorded feature; either default the interval to 2 or skip the comparison silently
framework_bugs.actual: Prints '[OK] Feature recorded (1/null until next test session)' followed by 'scripts/test-gate.sh: line 122: [: null: integer expression expected'. Exit code is still 0 but stderr contains a bash error that pollutes logs and confuses operators.

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 enforcement requires phase2_init steps to be marked complete first, otherwise gate denies with the misleading 'Phase 2 initialization not verified' reason instead of 'no Build Loop active'. The dependency should be documented in builders-guide.md Build Loop section.
documentation_gaps.where: docs/builders-guide.md (Build Loop / Phase 2 init relationship) and scripts/process-checklist.sh require_build_loop_state_for_commit

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: exit=0 with '[WARN] 1 feature(s) since last UAT session' (expected because we only ran one feature without test session). Gate proceeds with warning + attestation note.

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: lint-uat-scenarios.sh on the unfilled templates/uat/test-session-template.html produced 21 violations for '__SCENARIOS_JSON__', '__FEATURE_OPTIONS__', '__SESSION_DATE__' etc. (Expected — template needs population by orchestrator before lint.) Lint correctly identifies unreplaced placeholders. Marked false because runbook expects clean exit, but a generated/populated scenario is required to make this pass; framework does not provide a generator step.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS completed exit=0 in order

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: n/a for sponsored POC — Phase 4 is intentionally blocked until upgrade-project.sh --to-production

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook assertion 'uat_template_lints_clean' references generated UAT scenarios, but the framework provides only a template (templates/uat/test-session-template.html) with placeholders the linter (correctly) rejects. There's no scripted way to populate the template — orchestrator workflow (replace __SCENARIOS_JSON__, __FEATURE_OPTIONS__, __SESSION_DATE__, __SESSION_FEATURES__) is undocumented for builders.
documentation_gaps.where: templates/uat/test-session-template.html + scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh; need a 'generate-uat-template.sh' or builders-guide entry

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: Correctly blocked with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in sponsored poc mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3. To unlock Phase 4: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production'. Exit=1 is the EXPECTED behavior for Sponsored POC scenarios — pass = correct enforcement.

summary: Sponsored POC + web + full track end-to-end UAT completed via manual fallback (init.sh hang reproduced, 1 High bug). Phases 1-3 exercised cleanly via process-checklist.sh; BL-006 and BL-015 gates fire correctly. Phase 4 correctly blocked for Sponsored POC. 1 Medium bug (test-gate.sh null test_interval bash error). Overall_pass=false due to High-severity init.sh scriptability gap (expected systemic finding per runbook).
